headline: Best action picks to stream when you want something better than another lazy scroll
meta_description: Need better action picks for tonight? Here are the titles actually worth starting first, without the usual generic streaming-list fluff.
subtitle: Featuring thefamily man, Dunkirk, and Deepwater Horizon — plus more action picks that deserve real attention.

hero_image.title: Best action picks to stream when you want something better than another lazy scroll
hero_image.subtitle: Featuring thefamily man, Dunkirk, and Deepwater Horizon — plus more action picks that deserve real attention.

selected_titles.title: thefamily man
selected_titles.description: A middle-class man and world-class spy, struggles to balance family life with his role in the National Intelligence Agency.

selected_titles.title: Dunkirk
selected_titles.description: The evacuation of Allied soldiers during WWII.

selected_titles.title: Deepwater Horizon
selected_titles.description: An oil rig faces a catastrophic explosion.

selected_titles.title: The Siege of Jadotville
selected_titles.description: This war drama showcases the untold story of Irish UN troops in Congo, delivering intense action and a reminder of the power of strategic thinking over brute force.

selected_titles.title: American Sniper
selected_titles.description: A Navy SEAL becomes a top sniper in U.S. history.

selected_titles.title: John Wick
selected_titles.description: An ex-hitman comes out of retirement to track down the gangsters who took everything from him.

selected_titles.title: Khakee
selected_titles.description: A Hindi action-thriller about a police squad tasked with escorting a terrorist to court.

cta.headline: Want a custom watchlist instead of generic streaming lists?
cta.body: Message MovieJam on WhatsApp and get 3 tailored picks based on your mood, genre, and how much brainpower you want to spend tonight.

internal_link_defaults.title: How to get custom movie recs on WhatsApp
Why endless scrolling ruins movie night

style_rules: Lead with a viral, opinionated hook in the first paragraph.
Keep the same vibe as the existing MyMovieJam blog: sharp, audience-first, clean, punchy, and honest.
Write like a real viewer, not a neutral encyclopedia.
Do not fake plot details, awards, or availability.
If the CSV data is thin, make the opinion narrower instead of inventing facts.
Use the MyMovieJam visual theme and CTA style already present on the site.
SEO first, but never at the cost of sounding robotic.
Use clear subheads, quick picks, FAQs, and a strong WhatsApp CTA.
notes_for_writer: Tell the truth if the list mixes films and series.
Write as a sharp viewer with taste, not a neutral database.
Pick clear winners, easy starters, and mood-based recommendations.
Use a clear WhatsApp CTA at the end.
